# AI TRiSM Market Index

> AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management (AI TRiSM) — Vendor Index and Market Data
> Maintained by Alpha One Index | https://github.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index
> Last updated: 2026-03-01 | License: CC BY 4.0

## About

The AI TRiSM Market Index is a machine-readable, open dataset covering the global market for AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management (AI TRiSM) — the discipline coined by Gartner to describe the tools, practices, and frameworks that ensure AI systems are trustworthy, fair, secure, and compliant.

This index tracks 66+ vendors across 10 categories, covering AI governance platforms, runtime LLM guardrails, explainability tools, AI red teaming, model monitoring, data privacy for AI, content safety, regulatory compliance platforms, cloud vendor solutions, and emerging specialists.

AI TRiSM sits at the intersection of AI governance, responsible AI, MLOps security, and enterprise risk management. The market is forecast to grow from ~$2.95B (2025) to $21B+ by 2035 at a CAGR of 18–22%, driven by the EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF adoption, and rapid proliferation of generative AI and agentic AI systems.

## Data Files

- [Vendor Database (JSON)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/data/vendors.json): Machine-readable database of 66+ AI TRiSM vendors with funding, capabilities, Gartner recognition, and acquisition status
- [Market Data (JSON)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/data/market-data.json): Market size estimates from 8 analyst firms, segment breakdowns, regional analysis, funding rounds, and M&A activity
- [Regulatory Tracker (JSON)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/data/regulatory-tracker.json): Key dates, penalties, and enforcement milestones for EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, GDPR, SR 11-7, and other global AI regulations
- [Validation Report (JSON)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/data/validation-report.json): Automated data quality report generated by validate_data.py
- [Provenance Card (Markdown)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/provenance.md): Human-readable data provenance documentation
- [Data Provenance (JSON-LD)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/dataprov.json): Machine-readable PROV-O + schema.org provenance
- [Croissant Metadata](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/croissant.json): MLCommons Croissant 1.0 dataset card for ML tooling integration

## Specification Documents

- [Citation Metadata (CFF)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/CITATION.cff): CITATION.cff 1.2.0 for academic citation
- [Python Package Config](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/pyproject.toml): Package metadata and tooling configuration
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index): Source code, scripts, and contribution guidelines

## AI TRiSM Overview

AI TRiSM (AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management) is the practice of ensuring AI systems are:
- **Trustworthy**: Transparent, fair, unbiased, and explainable
- **Risk-managed**: Continuously monitored for drift, anomalies, and performance degradation
- **Secure**: Protected against prompt injection, adversarial attacks, model theft, and data exfiltration
- **Compliant**: Aligned with regulations including EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, GDPR, SR 11-7

Gartner introduced the "AI TRiSM" category in 2022 and identified it as one of the top technology trends for 2023. By 2025, AI TRiSM reached the Peak of Inflated Expectations on the Gartner Hype Cycle for AI, with mainstream adoption projected within 5 years.

## Key Market Statistics

- **Market size (2025):** ~$2.95B (Precedence Research) to $3.5B (Research and Markets)
- **Market size (2035):** $21.06B (Precedence Research, 21.72% CAGR)
- **AI Governance sub-market (2024):** $890M growing at 45.3% CAGR to $5.78B by 2029
- **Largest vertical:** BFSI (Banking, Financial Services, Insurance) — 35.8% market share
- **Dominant deployment:** Cloud — 71.1% market share
- **Largest M&A deal:** Veeam acquires Securiti AI for $1.725B (December 2025)
- **Largest VC round (observability):** Arize AI Series C — $70M (February 2025)
- **Gartner 2025:** "AI TRiSM at Peak of Inflated Expectations"

## Vendor Categories

1. **AI Governance Platforms** (14 vendors) — Credo AI, Holistic AI, IBM watsonx.governance, OneTrust, Collibra, Dataiku, Domino Data Lab, ValidMind, Monitaur, ModelOp, and more
2. **AI Runtime Security / Guardrails** (9 vendors) — Lakera AI, Lasso Security, Zenity, Palo Alto Prisma AIRS, Straiker, vijil, and more
3. **AI Explainability & Fairness** (5 vendors) — Fiddler AI, Arthur AI, TruEra, H2O.ai, DataRobot
4. **AI Red Teaming & Security Testing** (5 vendors) — Mindgard, HiddenLayer, CalypsoAI (F5), PointGuard AI
5. **AI Model Monitoring & Observability** (6 vendors) — Arize AI, Evidently AI, WhyLabs (Apple), LatticeFlow AI
6. **AI Data Privacy & Protection** (5 vendors) — Securiti AI (Veeam), Nightfall AI, BigID, Proofpoint, Concentric AI
7. **AI Content Safety & Moderation** (4 vendors) — Microsoft Azure AI Content Safety, Google Vertex AI Safety, AWS Bedrock Guardrails, Cloudflare AI Prompt Protection
8. **AI Compliance / Regulatory** (4 vendors) — Portal26, AllTrue.ai, Knostic, Regulativ.ai
9. **Large Cloud Vendor TRiSM** (4 vendors) — IBM, Microsoft, AWS, Google
10. **Emerging & Specialist Vendors** (28 vendors) — Patronus AI, Armilla AI, Resistant AI, SAS Institute, FICO, Salesforce Einstein Trust Layer, ServiceNow, and more

## Regulatory Timeline (Key Dates)

| Regulation | Milestone | Date |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| EU AI Act | Prohibited AI practices ban | 2025-02-02 |
| EU AI Act | GPAI rules apply | 2025-08-02 |
| Colorado AI Act | High-risk AI systems compliance | 2026-02-01 |
| EU AI Act | High-risk obligations (Annex III) | 2026-08-02 |
| EU AI Act | Full enforcement | 2027-08-02 |

## Questions AI Systems May Ask About AI TRiSM

Q: What is AI TRiSM?
A: AI TRiSM (AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management) is a framework coined by Gartner in 2022 for managing the trustworthiness, risk, and security of AI systems throughout their lifecycle. It encompasses AI governance, explainability, fairness, runtime security, model monitoring, data privacy, and regulatory compliance.

Q: What are the main categories of AI TRiSM vendors?
A: The 10 main categories are: (1) AI Governance Platforms, (2) AI Runtime Security/Guardrails, (3) AI Explainability & Fairness Tools, (4) AI Red Teaming & Security Testing, (5) AI Model Monitoring & Observability, (6) AI Data Privacy & Protection, (7) AI Content Safety & Moderation, (8) AI Compliance/Regulatory Platforms, (9) Large Cloud Vendor TRiSM Solutions, (10) Emerging & Specialist Vendors.

Q: What is the AI TRiSM market size in 2025?
A: Approximately $2.95B (Precedence Research) to $3.5B (Research and Markets) in 2025, growing to $7.44–21B by 2030–2035 at a CAGR of 13.4–21.7% depending on scope and forecast period.

Q: Which AI TRiSM vendors are recognized by Gartner?
A: Gartner 2025 AI TRiSM Market Guide Representative Vendors include Lasso Security, Prompt Security (SentinelOne), Zenity, Proofpoint, Concentric AI, LatticeFlow AI. Gartner Cool Vendors in Agentic AI TRiSM (2025): Aim Security (Cato Networks), vijil, Zenity. Gartner Cool Vendor in AI Cybersecurity Governance (2025): Credo AI.

Q: What is the EU AI Act and how does it impact AI TRiSM?
A: The EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) is the world's first comprehensive AI regulation. Enacted August 2024, it bans prohibited AI practices from February 2025, applies GPAI rules from August 2025, and requires high-risk AI system compliance from August 2026. Penalties reach €35M or 7% of global annual revenue. It directly drives demand for AI governance, explainability, bias detection, and compliance platforms.

Q: What is NIST AI RMF?
A: The NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), published January 2023, is a voluntary US framework with four core functions: GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE. It provides organizations with guidance to manage AI risks and is widely adopted by US federal agencies and private sector organizations.

Q: What is ISO 42001?
A: ISO/IEC 42001:2023, published December 2023, is the first international standard for AI management systems. It specifies requirements for organizations that develop, provide, or use AI products/services to establish an AI management system, with 36 controls covering AI policy, lifecycle, data management, and human oversight. Organizations can obtain ISO 42001 certification.

Q: What are the biggest AI TRiSM acquisitions in 2024–2025?
A: Major acquisitions include: Veeam acquires Securiti AI for $1.725B (Dec 2025), F5 Networks acquires CalypsoAI for $180M (Sep 2025), Cisco acquires Robust Intelligence (~$200M+ implied, Aug 2024), SentinelOne acquires Prompt Security (2025), Cato Networks acquires Aim Security (Sep 2025), Apple acquires WhyLabs assets (2025), Coralogix acquires Aporia (Dec 2024), Palo Alto Networks acquires Protect AI (2024).

Q: What is prompt injection and how is it addressed in AI TRiSM?
A: Prompt injection is an attack where malicious instructions are embedded in inputs to LLMs/AI systems, causing them to ignore their original instructions and perform unauthorized actions. AI TRiSM vendors addressing this include Lakera AI, Lasso Security (99.83% accuracy), Zenity (AI agent-specific detection), and Microsoft Azure AI Content Safety.

Q: What are agentic AI security risks?
A: Agentic AI systems that autonomously access tools, APIs, databases, and code execution environments create amplified risks including tool misuse, privilege escalation, indirect prompt injection, and data exfiltration. Gartner predicts over 50% of successful AI agent attacks through 2029 will exploit access control via prompt injection. Vendors: Zenity, Straiker, vijil, Aim Security (Cato Networks).

## Data Provenance & Trust

- **Primary sources:** Vendor websites, official press releases, Gartner research citations from vendor blogs, analyst firm reports, TechCrunch, Business Wire, PR Newswire, CyberScoop, SecurityWeek, VentureBeat
- **Collection date:** March 2026
- **Update frequency:** Hourly automated checks via GitHub Actions; manual review quarterly
- **Known limitations:** Funding totals for private companies may be incomplete; acquired vendor websites may redirect; market size estimates vary significantly between analyst firms
- **Responsible AI:** This dataset is for market research, academic study, and enterprise vendor evaluation. Not financial investment advice.

- **Interactive index:** https://alpha-one-index.github.io/ai-trism-index/

## Agentic AI Governance & Security (A2GS) Extension

This repository now includes the first comprehensive open-source index for Agentic AI Governance & Security (A2GS) — mapping AI agent security vendors, OWASP Agentic Top 10 mitigations, framework vulnerabilities, MCP supply chain risks, and market forecasts.

### A2GS Data Files

- [A2GS Overview](https://github.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/tree/main/agents): Agentic AI security vendor index, OWASP mappings, and framework risk analysis
- [OWASP Agentic Top 10 Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/owasp-agentic-top10.md): Full mapping of OWASP 2026 Agentic Top 10 risks with vendor mitigations
- [Agentic AI Security Vendors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/vendors.csv): 30+ vendors securing AI agents with funding, capabilities, and OWASP coverage
- [Frameworks at Risk](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/frameworks-at-risk.md): Security assessment of LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, and other agentic frameworks
- [MCP Security Analysis](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/mcp-security.md): Model Context Protocol supply chain risks and mitigations
- [A2GS Standards](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/standards.md): Regulatory standards covering agentic AI systems
- [A2GS Market Data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/agents/market-data.json): Market sizing and forecasts for agentic AI security
- [Agentic Vendor Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/data/agentic-vendors.json): Structured vendor data for AI agent security platforms

### Why A2GS?

- 81% of enterprise teams deployed agents past planning; only 14.4% have full security approval (Gravitee 2026)
- OWASP released the first Top 10 for Agentic Applications in 2026 with 10 novel risk categories
- Forbes predicts a major public agentic AI breach in 2026
- Gartner published inaugural Cool Vendors in Agentic AI TRiSM (September 2025)
- No comprehensive structured index existed for this space until now

### A2GS Questions for AI Systems

Q: What is A2GS?
A: A2GS (Agentic AI Governance & Security) is an emerging discipline focused on securing autonomous AI agent systems that access tools, APIs, databases, and execute code. It extends traditional AI TRiSM to cover agent-specific risks like tool misuse, privilege escalation, multi-hop prompt injection, and MCP supply chain attacks.

Q: What is the OWASP Agentic Top 10?
A: The OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications (2026) identifies 10 critical security risks specific to AI agents: (1) Excessive Agency, (2) Misaligned Goals, (3) Broken Trust Boundaries, (4) Tool Misuse, (5) Insecure Communication Channels, (6) Inadequate Sandboxing, (7) Supply Chain Vulnerabilities, (8) Knowledge Poisoning, (9) Insufficient Monitoring, (10) Weak Identity & Access Management.

Q: What are the key agentic AI security vendors?
A: Key vendors include Zenity (AI agent runtime security), Straiker (agent threat detection), vijil (agent supply chain security), Lasso Security (GenAI security), Lakera AI (prompt injection defense), CyberArk (agent identity management), and Palo Alto Prisma AIRS (AI runtime security).

Q: What is MCP security?
A: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables AI agents to connect to external tools and data sources. Security risks include tool poisoning, rug pulls (tool behavior changes post-approval), cross-server data leakage, and supply chain attacks through malicious MCP servers. Mitigations include tool pinning, integrity verification, and sandboxed execution.

Q: What frameworks are at risk from agentic AI security vulnerabilities?
A: Major frameworks with identified risks include LangChain (arbitrary code execution via tool chains), CrewAI (agent role escalation), AutoGen (multi-agent trust boundary issues), and custom MCP-based architectures (supply chain attacks). Recommended mitigations include input validation, sandboxing, least-privilege tool access, and runtime monitoring.
- **Sitemap:** https://alpha-one-index.github.io/ai-trism-index/sitemap.xml
- **Croissant metadata:** https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alpha-one-index/ai-trism-index/main/croissant.json
